identity.short_name: Probability an ambiguous 12-hour configuration has error \leq5 (output A+B)

subdomain(s): fractional part constraints on real variables
counting on finite sets and reduced fractions

problem_spec.summary: On a 12-hour clock with indistinguishable hour/minute hands, ambiguous configurations are intersections of the validity curve m=12{h} with its swap. Decompose by integer parts to obtain a discrete family indexed by n=|a-b|, count how many configurations have error E=|h-m| at most a given window, and compute the resulting probability and the sum of numerator and denominator.
givens: The clock is accurate, but the hour and minute hands are indistinguishable.
A valid ordered reading (h,m) with 0<=h,m<12 satisfies m = 12*{h}.
A configuration is ambiguous if both (h,m) and (m,h) are valid and h\neqm.
Choose an ambiguous configuration uniformly at random from all ambiguous configurations in a 12-hour cycle.
ask: Let p be the probability that E<=5. Write p=A/B in lowest terms and output out=A+B.

parameter(s).name: cycle_length
parameter(s).purpose: hour-cycle length for the clock model

parameter(s).name: selection_model
parameter(s).purpose: defines the probability space (uniform over all ambiguous configurations)

invariant(s).name: valid_time_pair_constraint
invariant(s).statement: A valid ordered clock reading satisfies m = 12{h} with 0<=h,m<12.

invariant(s).name: ambiguity_via_swap
invariant(s).statement: An ambiguous configuration satisfies both m=12{h} and h=12{m}, with h\neqm.

invariant(s).name: discrete_error_levels
invariant(s).statement: Writing a=floor(h), b=floor(m), ambiguity solutions satisfy E=|h-m|=12|a-b|/13, so E takes discrete values indexed by n=|a-b|.

invariant(s).name: configuration_count_by_difference
invariant(s).statement: For n=1,...,11, the number of ambiguous configurations with |a-b|=n is 12-n, and the total number of ambiguous configurations is C(12,2)=66.

invariant(s).name: probability_is_favorable_over_total
invariant(s).statement: Under uniform selection over ambiguous configurations, p = (# configurations with E<=window) / 66.

essential_assumption(s): Times are modeled continuously within one 12-hour cycle (0<=h,m<12).
Ambiguous configurations exclude the trivial case h=m.
The probability space is uniform over the finite set of ambiguous configurations.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Decomposing by integer parts a=floor(h), b=floor(m) yields a unique solution (h,m) for each unordered pair {a,b} with a\neqb, and the error E depends only on n=|a-b|. The window E<=5 uniquely determines which n are favorable, and counting such pairs produces a unique reduced probability p=A/B and hence a unique out=A+B.

technique(s).name: set_up_fractional_part_equations
technique(s).purpose: translate validity and swap ambiguity into equations involving fractional parts

technique(s).name: decomposition_by_integer_parts
technique(s).purpose: introduce a=floor(h), b=floor(m) to linearize the system on each unit square

technique(s).name: derive_discrete_offsets
technique(s).purpose: solve for h,m and derive E=12|a-b|/13

technique(s).name: count_configurations_by_difference
technique(s).purpose: count ambiguous configurations with each n=|a-b| and total configurations

technique(s).name: finalize_fraction_output
technique(s).purpose: compute p as reduced fraction and output A+B
